author: anton
description: Decorate exported Apple Notes and Bear Notes with native app icons. Open notes in their source app. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Alto Computer
manifest.description: Sync Apple Notes, Bear, and Logseq into your vault as plain Markdown — sync status, native app icons, and deep links back to the source app.
manifest.name: Notes Exporter

name: Notes Exporter

releases.author: podviaznikov
releases.description:
Passes the Obsidian community directory scan.

- declare minAppVersion 1.7.2 (we call revealLeaf, @since 1.7.2)
- popout-window safe: activeDocument and window.setTimeout
- replace deprecated activeLeaf with getActiveViewOfType
- list @codemirror/view and @codemirror/state as dependencies
- narrow frontmatter and loadData() off `any`

releases.author: podviaznikov
releases.description:
## Notes Exporter 0.2.0

The plugin now does real work inside Obsidian instead of just decorating links:

- **Sync status panel** — new sidebar view (ribbon icon or "Show sync status" command) showing every export in your vault: notes exported, last sync time, and any failures with direct links to the affected notes. Auto-refreshes while open.
- **Logseq support** — the companion Mac app now exports Logseq graphs too; logseq:// deep links get icons, "Open in Logseq" menu items, and status bar info, same as Apple Notes and Bear.
- **Getting-started guide** — vaults without exported notes see a short setup walkthrough.
- Under the hood: removed the page-wide DOM observer (lighter on large vaults), general cleanup.

Works with [Notes Exporter for Mac](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/notes-exporter/id6741618455?mt=12). Frontmatter dates are now written timezone-less so Obsidian recognizes `created`/`modified` as real Date & time properties (app 3.6+).
